description: FOUR EAST HARTFORD GAZETTETHURSDAY JULY 26 Avoiding Air Collisions The crash of two huge airliners over the Grand Canyon recently emphasizes the disturb ing fact that Civil Aeronautics Administration air traffic rules are hopelessly obsolete The investigation which has followed this crash attempts to put the blame on the TWA pilot becauseit is revealed he was instruct ed to look out for other planes The United Airlines pilot had a flight clearance and priority to fly at 21000 feet on an instrument flight plan That means for those who do not under stand flight plans that the United Airlines pilot was flying on instruments and had clearance to fly at the 21000foot altitude on instruments and no obligation to watch out for oncoming planes The TWA Airlines pilot it is said had only a VFR clearance which means a clearance based on the pilots vision primarily and not navigational instruments Therefore theoretically he was supposed to look out for other planes The hopeless obso lescence of such a system is pointed up by the fact that these two planes were both capable of flying approximately 300 miles an hour The two commercial airliners which collided recently might have been closing
